rnv6: Decouple cadence — pin Hermes + Yoga to their OWN upstream coordinates, independent of the React-Native release train. canopy/native does NOT use Fabric, so there is no reason to ride RN's cadence. This file is the machine-readable source of truth for those independent pins; scripts/revendor.sh reads it; host/android/vendor/CADENCE-PIN.md is the human decision record.

policy.rationale: Owner's explicit decision. No Fabric => no reason to bump on RN's cadence. Re-review the independent Hermes/Yoga pins twice a year (the scripts/bump-check.sh cron surfaces newer upstreams continuously; this is the human gate that decides whether to actually adopt). A bump is adopted only when it is ABI-verified end-to-end (scripts/check-abi.sh green) and the C++ pin moves in lockstep.

android.source: Maven Central (facebook/yoga standalone release, NOT react-android)
android.note: Already fully decoupled from RN on Android: Yoga is its own Maven coordinate; the libyoga.so + Java bindings come from facebook/yoga, not from the react-android AAR.

ios.podFallback: Yoga.podspec (RN node_modules) — the CocoaPods primary path today
ios.source: facebook/yoga standalone (Swift Package Manager); see host/ios/Package.swift
ios.note: iOS can take Yoga from its own SPM coordinate (Package.swift already pins facebook/yoga). The pod path stays primary until proven on a Mac (Mac-gated).

hermes.decoupleVehicleCadence: Hermes' OWN version line (v0.x), independent of RN's 0.7x line

cabiVtable.finding: NO publicly-distributed Hermes prebuilt — neither RN's hermes-android AAR (0.76.9 through 0.82.1) NOR facebook/hermes's standalone GitHub-release runtime — exports get_hermes_abi_vtable. The C-ABI vtable (HermesABIRuntimeWrapper / get_hermes_abi_vtable) is compiled in only when Hermes is built from source with the C-ABI option enabled; it is absent from every prebuilt and there is no separately-published `hermesabi` Maven artifact.
cabiVtable.consequence: RNV-4 backend (B) (-DCANOPY_HERMES_CABI, makeHermesABIRuntimeWrapper(get_hermes_abi_vtable())) cannot be the default from a PREBUILT. The durable C-vtable flip is gated on a from-source Hermes build (or an upstream prebuilt that flips this flag). scripts/check-hermes-cabi.sh probes this on every CI run and flips its NOTICE to CAPABILITY the day a vendored libhermes exports the vtable.
cabiVtable.flipTrigger: When scripts/revendor.sh cadence (or a from-source build) installs a libhermes.so that exports get_hermes_abi_vtable on BOTH ABIs, set this to true, build with -DCANOPY_HERMES_CABI=1, and make backend (B) the default in host/shared/cpp/CanopyHermes.cpp.

hermes.activePinNote: TODAY the host links the ABI-matched Hermes vendored under host/android/vendor/lib (currently react-native 0.76.9's hermes-android; see host/vendor.lock.json + scripts/check-abi.sh). This file records the INDEPENDENT decouple vehicle + policy; the active Hermes binary stays whatever the ABI gate proves matched. The standalone pin above is the proven, ready-to-pull alternative path (revendor.sh cadence --fetch-hermes), NOT yet the active default, because v0.11.0 would regress the bytecode version below RN 0.76.9's Hermes.
